sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.3g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 22.8g. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for fruit, the tradeoff is sweetness versus fibre, satiety, and overall payoff.

sections.title: Proteins

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 10% DV, vitamin a at 1% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 8% DV, magnesium at 7% DV, iron at 1% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. very practical everyday fruit because cheap, portable, and easy to use, which matters for a fruit people actually eat often. often easy to tolerate because a practical plus for many people compared with harsher foods. brings a useful potassium identity because not enough to make it elite, but still a real supportive strength.

closing.summary: Banana stands out for very practical everyday fruit and often easy to tolerate. but it also gets held back by sugar load is fairly high and fibre is only modest. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
